Role.title: Role
Role.description: Defines the different access levels and permissions within the UniEats system, such as Client, Cashier, Kitchen Staff, and Admin.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Role entity.

name.description: The name of the role (e.g., 'Client', 'Cashier', 'Kitchen Staff', 'Admin').

description.description: A brief explanation of the responsibilities and permissions associated with this role.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents an individual user of the UniEats application, including clients (students, teachers) and internal staff (cashiers, kitchen, administration). Assumes an external authentication system for credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The institutional email address of the user, used for login.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

roleId.description: Reference to the Role assigned to this user. (Relationship: Role 1:N User)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Categorizes menu items, helping to organize the menu for customers and staff (e.g., 'Bebidas', 'Dulces', 'Platos Principales').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the category (e.g., 'Main Courses', 'Drinks', 'Desserts').

description.description: A brief description of what this category includes.

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Represents a single item available for purchase from the cafeteria menu, including its details, price, and availability.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

name.description: The name of the menu item (e.g., 'Café Americano', 'Sandwich de Pollo').

description.description: A detailed description of the menu item, including ingredients or preparation style.

price.description: The selling price of the menu item.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image of the menu item.

categoryId.description: Reference to the category this menu item belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N MenuItem)

isAvailable.description: Indicates if the menu item is currently available for order.

preparationTimeMinutes.description: Estimated time in minutes required to prepare this item.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the menu item was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the menu item was last updated.

Ingredient.title: Ingredient
Ingredient.description: Represents a raw material or component used in the preparation of menu items, allowing for detailed inventory control.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ingredient entity.

name.description: The name of the ingredient (e.g., 'Café Grano', 'Leche Fresca', 'Pan de Molde').

unit.description: The unit of measure for this ingredient (e.g., 'grams', 'ml', 'unit', 'kg', 'liter').

description.description: A brief description of the ingredient.

Inventory.title: Inventory
Inventory.description: Tracks the current stock levels of individual ingredients, facilitating inventory control and low-stock alerts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inventory record.

ingredientId.description: Reference to the Ingredient being tracked. (Relationship: Ingredient 1:N Inventory)

currentStock.description: The current quantity of the ingredient in stock, measured in its defined unit.

minStockLevel.description: The minimum stock level for this ingredient, triggering a low-stock alert when reached.

lastRestockDate.description: Timestamp of the last time this ingredient was restocked.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the inventory record was last updated.

RecipeIngredient.title: RecipeIngredient
RecipeIngredient.description: A linking entity that defines the quantity of each ingredient required for a specific menu item, forming its recipe. (Relationship: MenuItem N:N Ingredient)

id.description: Unique identifier for the RecipeIngredient record.

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem for which this ingredient is used. (Relationship: MenuItem N:N Ingredient)

ingredientId.description: Reference to the Ingredient used in the menu item. (Relationship: MenuItem N:N Ingredient)

quantityNeeded.description: The quantity of the ingredient required to prepare one unit of the menu item.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's placed order, containing overall details like total amount, status, and pickup information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the user who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

orderDateTime.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

pickupTime.description: The customer-selected date and time for order pickup.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary value of the order.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'preparing', 'ready_for_pickup', 'completed', 'cancelled').

queueNumber.description: An optional number indicating the order's position in the preparation queue, for customer visibility.

specialInstructions.description: Any specific instructions or requests from the customer regarding the order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was last updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a specific menu item included within an order, detailing its quantity and any customizations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the parent order. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

menuItemId.description: Reference to the menu item included in this order item. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the menu item ordered.

itemPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the menu item at the time the order was placed.

customizations.description: A string representing any special customizations for this specific item (e.g., 'sin cebolla', 'extra queso').

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Stores customer feedback and ratings for specific menu items, contributing to recommendations and quality improvement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

userId.description: Reference to the user who submitted the review. (Relationship: User 1:N Review)

menuItemId.description: Reference to the menu item being reviewed. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N Review)

rating.description: The numerical rating given to the menu item, typically on a scale (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

comment.description: Optional textual feedback or comment from the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the review was submitted.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for admin users. Document ID is the user's UID. Used for explicit role-based access control checks (DBAC) using `exists()` in security rules.
Dedicated collection for cashier users. Document ID is the user's UID. Used for explicit role-based access control checks (DBAC) using `exists()` in security rules.
Dedicated collection for kitchen staff users. Document ID is the user's UID. Used for explicit role-based access control checks (DBAC) using `exists()` in security rules.
Stores user profiles. Each document is owned by the corresponding user (path-based ownership). Readable by the user themselves and by admin users.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

definition.description: Defines menu item categories. Publicly readable for clients to browse the menu, admin managed for creation, editing, and deletion.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the menu category.

definition.description: Stores details for each menu item, including name, description, price, and availability. Publicly readable for clients, admin managed.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the menu item.

definition.description: Defines raw materials and components used in menu item recipes. Restricted to admin and kitchen staff.

params.name: ingredientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the ingredient.

definition.description: Tracks the current stock levels of ingredients. Restricted to admin and kitchen staff. Includes denormalized 'ingredientId' for direct querying.

params.name: inventoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the inventory record.

definition.description: Links menu items to their required ingredients and quantities. Restricted to admin and kitchen staff.

params.name: recipeIngredientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the recipe ingredient record.

definition.description: Primary collection for client-specific orders. Contains full order details. Access is restricted to the owning client (userId in path) and admin. Includes denormalized 'userId' for ownership checks and 'status', 'queueNumber' for overall order state.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who placed the order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order.

definition.description: Subcollection storing individual items for a specific order. Inherits ownership from the parent order. Includes 'menuItemId', 'quantity', and 'itemPrice'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who placed the parent order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order item.

definition.description: Denormalized view of orders for kitchen staff. Contains essential order details like 'status', 'queueNumber', 'pickupTime', 'orderDateTime', 'specialInstructions', and a summary of ordered items. Read/write access is limited to kitchen staff (for status updates) and admin. This collection ensures QAPs for kitchen staff's 'list' operations.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order (matches the ID in the user's orders).

definition.description: Denormalized, minimal view of orders for the public display screen. Contains only 'queueNumber', 'status', and 'pickupTime'. This collection is world-readable to allow anonymous users to view the queue status, ensuring QAPs for public access.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order (matches the ID in the user's orders).

definition.description: Stores customer reviews for menu items. Writable by authenticated users for their own reviews, readable by all. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier of the review.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Query as Permission (QAP). 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

*   **User Roles (DBAC):** Instead of relying on `User.roleId` for real-time authorization checks (which would require a `get()` on the user document), dedicated collections such as `/roles_admin/{userId}`, `/roles_cashier/{userId}`, and `/roles_kitchen_staff/{userId}` are used. Authorization rules can perform a simple `exists()` check on these collections, providing atomic and performant role-based access control without hierarchical dependencies.
*   **Orders:** To facilitate distinct access patterns for clients, kitchen staff, and public displays, order data is denormalized across three main collections:
    1.  `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`: This is the primary, user-owned record of an order, containing all details. It ensures that clients can only access their own orders (path-based ownership). The `OrderItem` subcollection here further clarifies this ownership.
    2.  `/kitchen_orders/{orderId}`: A denormalized subset of order data specifically tailored for kitchen staff. It includes `status`, `queueNumber`, `pickupTime`, `specialInstructions`, and a summary of items (e.g., `orderItemsSummary`). This collection's homogenous security posture (only kitchen staff can list/update relevant orders) enables simple QAPs for kitchen staff without them needing access to all user-specific data or performing complex joins.
    3.  `/public_queue/{orderId}`: A minimal, denormalized view of orders (`queueNumber`, `status`, `pickupTime`) designed for public display screens. This collection is world-readable, again ensuring a homogenous security posture and enabling simple QAPs for anonymous users to view the queue.

This denormalization for orders is critical. It eliminates `get()` calls in security rules to check parent order status or client ownership when kitchen staff or public screens need to access data. Updates to an order (e.g., status change by kitchen staff) will trigger Cloud Functions to synchronize the relevant fields across the `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`, `/kitchen_orders/{orderId}`, and `/public_queue/{orderId}` documents.

**Structural Segregation for QAPs:**

*   **Homogeneous Security Posture:** Each collection or subcollection is designed with a specific security posture in mind, simplifying security rules and enabling efficient queries (QAPs).
    *   `/menu_items` and `/categories` are globally readable for clients, but admin-writable.
    *   `/users/{userId}` ensures path-based ownership for private user data.
    *   `/ingredients`, `/inventory`, and `/recipe_ingredients` are restricted to administrative and kitchen staff for management.
    *   The explicit separation of `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`, `/kitchen_orders/{orderId}`, and `/public_queue/{orderId}` ensures that `list` operations (QAPs) are robust. For instance, kitchen staff can perform a simple `list` on `/kitchen_orders` filtered by `status` without violating QAPs by exposing unrelated user data. Clients perform `list` on their own orders via the `/users/{userId}/orders` path, which is inherently secure.

**Clarity of Intent & Debuggability:**

*   Consistent naming conventions (e.g., `userId`, `orderId`, `menuItemId`) and descriptive wildcards (e.g., `{userId}` not `{id}`) enhance readability and debuggability of rules.
*   The `status` field in `Order` (and its denormalized versions) explicitly models the Finite State Machine of an order, making rule logic for state transitions clear and manageable.

This architecture ensures that the different user types (clients, kitchen, admin, cashier, public) can efficiently and securely access only the data relevant to their roles and use cases, minimizing the complexity of Firestore security rules.
